title: Day 13 Quiz — Dynamic Routing and URL Params
description: Verify dynamic routes, useParams, navigation, optional chaining, and route param typing.

questions.prompt: URL route parameters from useParams are always strings.
questions.explanation: URL segments are strings. Parse to number when needed: Number(userId) or parseInt(userId, 10).

questions.prompt: Which route defines a dynamic userId parameter?
questions.explanation: React Router uses :paramName for dynamic URL segments.

questions.prompt: What does user?.bio ?? 'No bio' return when user.bio is an empty string ''?
questions.explanation: ?? only falls back for null or undefined. Empty string is valid and preserved. || would fall back for falsy values.

questions.prompt: Which enable SPA navigation without full page reload? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Link and useNavigate use client-side routing. Plain anchor tags cause full reload and lose SPA state.

questions.prompt: Read URL params with {{blank1}}. Define dynamic segment as :userId in the {{blank2}} config.
questions.explanation: useParams extracts param values; route path defines dynamic segments.
Optional chaining on null returns undefined; ?? falls back to 'Guest'.

options.text: To show Not Found UI for unmatched URLs

questions.explanation: Catch-all route matches any unmatched path — standard 404 handling in SPAs.

questions.prompt: Why must useEffect refetch when userId param changes?
questions.sampleAnswer: When user navigates from /users/1 to /users/2, the same UserProfilePage component instance may remount or update. useEffect with [userId] dependency refetches the correct profile for the new URL param.
questions.explanation: Same component, different params — dependency array drives refetch.
Generic param to useParams types the expected param keys.

questions.prompt: Where should CartProvider wrap relative to Router for cart on profile page?

options.text: Above Router so all routes access Context

questions.explanation: Provider must wrap all components that use useCart — typically at app root above routes.
